name: PEDS Exam 1 — Chapters 15-19: Growth, Development, Family & Hospitalization

questions.question: A 7-year-old child proudly completes a 50-piece puzzle and shows it to the nurse. According to Erikson's psychosocial theory, this behavior demonstrates which developmental stage?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): According to Erikson, preschool children (3–6 years) are in the Initiative vs Guilt stage. Successfully completing tasks like puzzles, schoolwork, or crafts builds a sense of competence, industry, and self-worth. The child's pride in finishing the puzzle is a classic example. A is incorrect because Trust vs Mistrust (0–1 year) focuses on consistent caregiving building hope. B is incorrect because Industry vs Inferiority (6–12 years) is about task mastery and school-age accomplishment. D is incorrect because Autonomy vs Shame and Doubt (1–3 years) is about independence and toilet training.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching parents about infant development. Which statements accurately describe the principles of growth and development? (Select all that apply.)
options: Cephalocaudal development means infants lift their heads before they can sit.
Proximodistal development means infants grasp with fingers before using whole hand.
Growth is quantitative and can be measured in height and weight.
Development proceeds from general to specific movements.
Infants have lower metabolic rates per kilogram than adults.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, C, D): A is correct — cephalocaudal means head-to-toe, so infants gain head control (2–3 months) before trunk control and sitting (6–8 months). C is correct — growth is measurable (weight, height, head circumference). D is correct — development moves from general (whole arm reach) to specific (pincer grasp). B is incorrect — proximodistal is center-to-periphery, so infants use whole hand grasp before fine pincer grasp with fingers. E is incorrect — infants have HIGHER metabolic rates per kg than adults, requiring more calories and fluids proportionally.

questions.question: A 4-year-old hospitalized child refuses to eat green peas, saying, 'They're green and yucky.' According to Piaget, this is an example of which cognitive characteristic?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Centration is a hallmark of Piaget's Preoperational stage (2–7 years). The child focuses on ONE characteristic (color = green) while ignoring others (taste, nutrition, texture). This is why the peas are 'yucky' based on color alone. A is incorrect — object permanence develops in Sensorimotor stage (~8–9 months). C is incorrect — conservation (understanding quantity doesn't change with appearance) is Concrete Operational (7–11 years). D is incorrect — formal operational (abstract/hypothetical thinking) begins at 11+ years.

questions.question: An infant weighed 7 pounds 11 ounces at birth. What is the expected weight at 12 months of age?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Birth weight typically doubles by 5–6 months and triples by 12 months. 7 lb 11 oz × 3 ≈ 23 pounds. This is a key milestone — failure to meet weight expectations may indicate nutritional or developmental concerns. A is incorrect (this is roughly double, not triple). C and D are incorrect — those would indicate overgrowth or obesity concerns.

questions.question: A family consists of a mother, her biological son, a father, and his biological daughter from a previous marriage. The couple has one child together. Which family structure best describes this? (Select all that apply.)
questions.rationale: Correct (A and D): A blended family (also called step-family) is formed when persons with children from previous relationships remarry or cohabitate. This family has children from both parents' prior relationships plus a shared child. B is incorrect — nuclear family is traditional (parents + their biological children only, no stepchildren). C is incorrect — extended family includes grandparents, aunts, uncles, etc. living together.

questions.question: A nurse is performing a Denver II developmental screening on a 2-year-old. Which statement about this screening is correct?
options: It is an IQ test that determines intelligence.
It screens for delays in personal-social, fine motor, language, and gross motor skills.
It should be performed only by licensed psychologists.
A low score indicates the child will not catch up developmentally.
questions.rationale: Correct (B): The Denver II (Denver Developmental Screening Test) screens four domains: personal-social, fine motor-adaptive, language, and gross motor. It identifies children NOT performing at age level — it is NOT an intelligence test. A is incorrect — Denver II is NOT an IQ test; it screens for developmental delays. C is incorrect — nurses and trained professionals can perform it. D is incorrect — low scores indicate need for follow-up and possible intervention, but children often catch up with support.

questions.question: Match each developmental task with the correct Erikson stage:
rows: Completing school projects and feeling proud
Saying 'no' to everything and wanting independence
Playing house with friends using imagination
Wondering 'Who am I?' and experimenting with roles
questions.rationale: Correct mapping: Industry (school projects) → school-age 6–12 yrs; Autonomy ('no', independence) → toddler 1–3 yrs; Initiative (fantasy play, 'playing house') → preschool 3–6 yrs; Identity ('Who am I?') → adolescent 12–18 yrs. Trust vs Mistrust is infant (0–1 yr) — not applicable here. This question tests knowledge of Erikson's age-specific stages.

questions.question: A 2-year-old plays with blocks in the same room as another child but does not interact or share toys. This play behavior is best described as:
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Parallel play is characteristic of toddlers (1–3 years). Children play beside each other with similar toys but do not interact or share. A is incorrect — cooperative play involves playing together with shared goals (preschool 3–6 yrs). C is incorrect — associative play is early group play with some interaction (late preschool). D is incorrect — competitive play with rules and teams is school-age (7+ years).

questions.question: Which developmental milestones are expected in a 12-month-old infant? (Select all that apply.)
questions.rationale: Correct (A, C, D): A is correct — pincer grasp develops 9–12 months. C is correct — first meaningful words (mama, dada) appear ~10–12 months. D is correct — stranger anxiety peaks 6–8 months and continues through 12 months. B is incorrect — most infants cruise or take first steps 9–12 months, but independent walking is typically 12–15 months. E is incorrect — posterior fontanelle closes by 2–3 months.

questions.question: A 10-month-old hospitalized infant is fussy and restless. Which toy would be most developmentally appropriate?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Push-pull toys are ideal for 10-month-olds — they encourage locomotion (crawling, cruising) and support motor development. A is incorrect — modeling clay is for preschoolers (3–5 years). C is incorrect — board games with rules are for school-age children (6+ years). D is incorrect — complex building sets require fine motor skills beyond 10-month-olds.

questions.question: A parent asks how long time-out should last for their 3-year-old who hit a sibling. What is the appropriate duration?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Time-out duration is 1 minute per year of age. For a 3-year-old, that is 3 minutes. The child should be calm before timing begins, and no interaction or eye contact should occur during the time-out.

questions.question: Which statements about play types and developmental stages are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Preschoolers (3–5 years) engage in associative and cooperative play.
Toddlers (1–3 years) typically play beside but not with others.
School-age children (6–12 years) prefer fantasy and imaginary play.
Competitive play with rules is characteristic of school-age children.
Parallel play is the primary play type for infants under 12 months.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D): A is correct — preschoolers play house as family, take roles (cooperative/associative). B is correct — parallel play is toddlers. D is correct — school-age children play organized games with rules. C is incorrect — school-age focus on concrete tasks and peer relationships, not fantasy. E is incorrect — infants are in solitary play, not parallel play.

questions.question: A parent reports that their 3-month-old infant cannot lift their head when placed prone. What is the nurse's best response?
options: This is normal; head control develops by 6 months.
This finding should be reported to the healthcare provider.
The infant should be placed supine only to prevent SIDS.
This is expected if the baby was born prematurely.
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Infants should be able to lift their head when prone by 2–3 months. Inability at 3 months is a red flag for possible neurological concern and should be reported. A is incorrect — head control should be present by 3 months, not 6. C is incorrect — supine for sleep does not replace tummy time for development. D is incorrect — even premature infants should show head control by corrected age.

questions.question: Match each family or play description with the correct classification:
rows: Parents remarry; each has children from prior relationships
A 2-year-old plays with blocks next to another child without interaction
A 4-year-old plays house with friends, assigning roles
A 9-year-old plays organized soccer with rules and teams
questions.rationale: Correct: Blended family (remarriage with stepchildren) → B. Parallel play (toddlers, side-by-side) → C. Cooperative play (preschool, role-play) → D. Competitive play (school-age, rules/teams) → E. A is incorrect — nuclear is biological parents + their children only.

questions.question: A parent asks when their infant will have all 20 baby teeth. What is the correct response?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Deciduous (baby) teeth total 20 and are typically complete by 2.5 years of age. The first tooth (usually lower central incisor) erupts around 6–10 months, and ~6 teeth are present by the first birthday. A is incorrect — 6 months is when first tooth erupts. B is incorrect — only ~6 teeth by 1 year. D is incorrect — all baby teeth are in by 2.5 years.

questions.question: Which statements about infant nutrition are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Solids should be introduced around 4–6 months of age.
Iron-fortified rice cereal is the recommended first food.
Whole cow's milk can be given at 6 months of age.
Honey should be avoided until after 1 year of age.
Breastfeeding should stop completely when solids begin.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D): A is correct — solids introduced 4–6 months when infant shows readiness. B is correct — iron-fortified rice cereal is first food (iron stores deplete by 6 months). D is correct — no honey <1 year (botulism risk). C is incorrect — no cow's milk until 12 months (causes iron-deficiency anemia, poor digestion). E is incorrect — breastfeeding continues alongside solids; gradual weaning.

questions.question: An infant with colic is crying excessively in the evening. Which intervention should the nurse recommend?
options: Place the infant supine and leave alone
Hold the infant prone over the arm and gently rock
Give the infant a large bottle of formula
Keep the infant in bright, noisy environment
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Colic interventions include prone positioning over the arm (gas relief), pacifier, back massage, white noise, and caregiver breaks. Colic usually resolves by 3 months. A is incorrect — infants should not be left alone while crying excessively. C is incorrect — overfeeding can worsen colic. D is incorrect — quiet, dim environment is soothing.

questions.question: A parent asks about oral health for their 8-month-old. Which teaching is correct?
options: Brush teeth starting at 2 years of age
Brush teeth at eruption and use fluoride after 6 months
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Brush at eruption (around 6 months) and use fluoride after 6 months if not in water. A is incorrect — brushing starts with first tooth. B is incorrect — fluoride after 6 months. D is incorrect — sharing utensils transmits cariogenic bacteria (cavities).

questions.question: Which statements about pediatric safety are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Unintentional injury is the leading cause of death after age 1 year.
Car seats should be rear-facing until the child is 1 year AND 20–22 pounds.
Accidents are rare in school-age children.
Teaching 'hot' and traffic safety begins in toddlerhood.
Obesity prevention includes limiting screen time and encouraging activity.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D, E): A is correct — unintentional injuries (MVA, drowning, poisoning) are #1 cause of death after 1 year. B is correct — rear-facing until 1yr AND 20-22lb minimum. D is correct — anticipatory guidance for toddlers includes 'hot,' traffic, stair safety. E is correct — obesity prevention: activity 60 min/day, limit screen time, family meals. C is incorrect — school-age children still at risk (bike, sports, strangers).

questions.question: A 4-year-old refuses to eat peas, saying 'They're green and I don't like green things.' This is an example of:
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Centration is a Preoperational stage characteristic (2–7 years). The child focuses on ONE feature (color = green) and ignores others (taste, nutrition). This is why peas are 'yucky' based on color alone. A is incorrect — conservation is Concrete Operational (7–11 yrs). C is incorrect — object permanence is Sensorimotor (~9 mo). D is incorrect — not a moral level (Piaget cognitive stage).

questions.question: A parent mentions their child was prescribed tetracycline for an infection. What is the nurse's priority response?
options: Tetracycline is the best antibiotic for children.
Tetracycline can cause permanent tooth staining in children.
Tetracycline should be taken with dairy products.
Tetracycline is safe for all ages.
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Tetracycline causes permanent tooth staining in developing teeth. It should NEVER be given to children under 8 years. A is incorrect — tetracycline is contraindicated in children. C is incorrect — dairy decreases tetracycline absorption. D is incorrect — tetracycline is contraindicated in children and pregnancy.

questions.question: A 2-year-old hospitalized toddler screams and reaches for parents when they leave. Which stage of separation anxiety is this?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Protest is the first stage of separation anxiety. The child cries loudly, calls for parents, and may hit or push away strangers. This is normal and indicates attachment. B is incorrect — detachment (third stage) appears as 'adjustment' but is actually emotional withdrawal. A is incorrect — despair (second stage) shows withdrawal and depression. D is incorrect — denial is not a standard stage.

questions.question: Which interventions help reduce stress for a hospitalized toddler? (Select all that apply.)
options: Encourage parents to stay with the child
Use therapeutic play to prepare for procedures
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D, E): A is correct — parent presence reduces separation anxiety. B is correct — routines provide security. D is correct — comfort items (blanket, stuffed animal) reduce stress. E is correct — therapeutic play helps children process and prepare. C is incorrect — use simple, concrete explanations at the child's level.

questions.question: A 4-year-old is admitted for surgery. Which activity would best help the child express feelings about hospitalization?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Modeling clay is expressive and therapeutic for preschoolers. It allows them to work through feelings about hospitalization, procedures, and fears. A is incorrect — passive activity doesn't allow expression. C is incorrect — school-age appropriate, not 4-year-old. D is incorrect — competitive play is for older children; may increase stress.

questions.question: A 9-month-old is crying, kicking, and difficult to console after a procedure. Which pain scale is most appropriate?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): FLACC (Face, Legs, Activity, Cry, Consolability) is for nonverbal infants and children. It scores 0–2 in each category for a total 0–10. A and D are incorrect — numeric and visual scales require verbal/cognitive ability (school-age+). B is incorrect — FACES is for children 3+ who can point to faces.

questions.question: Which statements about preparing children for procedures are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Explain procedures just before for toddlers (1–3 years).
Use medical jargon so children understand better.
Allow school-age children to handle equipment.
Adolescents benefit from written and oral information.
Preschoolers should be prepared days in advance.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, C, D): A is correct — toddlers: just before, simple words. C is correct — school-age children can touch equipment and ask questions. D is correct — adolescents: oral + written info, involvement in decisions. B is incorrect — use simple, concrete terms. E is incorrect — preschoolers: day before is fine, too early causes anxiety.

questions.question: A nurse is planning care for a 4-year-old hospitalized child. Which toy/activity is most appropriate for therapeutic play?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Modeling clay allows expressive, creative play for preschoolers. It helps children process feelings about illness and hospitalization. A is incorrect — push-pull toy is for 9–12 month infants. C is incorrect — competitive games are for school-age. D is incorrect — rattles are for infants.

questions.question: A 5-year-old points to a face on the FACES pain scale that shows a frown and tears. What does this indicate?
options: The child is not in pain
The child has moderate to severe pain
The child wants to go home
questions.rationale: Correct (B): The FACES scale uses faces from smiling (0, no pain) to crying/frowning (10, worst pain). A frown with tears indicates moderate to severe pain. This scale is appropriate for children 3+ years who can point to the face matching their pain. A is incorrect — smiling face = no pain. C and D are incorrect — not related to pain assessment.

questions.question: Which intervention is most effective for preventing childhood obesity?
options: Limit screen time and encourage 60 minutes of daily activity
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Obesity prevention includes physical activity (60 min/day), limiting screen time (≤2 hours), family meals, appropriate portions, and role modeling. A is incorrect — healthy snacks are appropriate; restriction causes rebound overeating. C is incorrect — vitamins don't prevent obesity. D is incorrect — daily weighing increases anxiety, not prevention.

questions.question: Which statements about toddler nutrition are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Food jags (only wanting one food) are normal.
Juice should be limited to 4–6 ounces per day.
Milk intake should exceed 32 ounces per day.
Picky eating is a sign of a disorder.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D): A is correct — food jags are normal toddler behavior. B is correct — excess juice causes dental caries and poor nutrition. D is correct — self-feeding builds autonomy (Erikson). C is incorrect — >24 oz milk/day causes iron-deficiency anemia. E is incorrect — picky eating is normal, not a disorder.

questions.question: A child weighs 20 kg. The ordered medication is 10 mg/kg/day divided every 8 hours. What is the dose per administration?
questions.rationale: Correct (A): Total daily dose = 10 mg/kg × 20 kg = 200 mg/day. Divided q8h (3 times) = 200 ÷ 3 = 66.6 mg per dose. B is incorrect — that's the total daily dose. C is incorrect — 100 mg would be for q12h. D is incorrect — 50 mg is too low.

questions.question: Which immunizations are typically given at 2 months of age?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): At 2 months, infants receive DTaP #1, IPV #1, Hib #1, HepB #2 (if not at birth), Rotavirus #1, PCV13 #1. A is incorrect — MMR and Varicella are at 12 months. C is incorrect — Tdap at 11–12 years, Meningococcal at 11–12 and 16 years. D is incorrect — flu starts at 6 months annually.

questions.question: A previously toilet-trained 3-year-old begins wetting the bed after hospitalization. What is the nurse's best response to the parents?
options: This is abnormal and needs immediate intervention.
This is normal regression; praise dry nights and ignore accidents.
The child is acting out and needs discipline.
This indicates a urinary tract infection.
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Regression (returning to earlier behaviors) is NORMAL during stress/illness/hospitalization. It resolves when stress is removed. Parents should praise appropriate behavior and ignore regression. A is incorrect — normal during stress. C is incorrect — not acting out, it's a coping mechanism. D is incorrect — not indicative of UTI without other symptoms.

questions.question: Which anticipatory guidance topics are appropriate for parents of a 6-month-old? (Select all that apply.)
options: Place baby on back to sleep
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D): A is correct — solids at 4–6 months. B is correct — back to sleep (SIDS prevention). D is correct — childproofing for mobile infant. C is incorrect — potty training is 18–24 months (toddler). E is incorrect — stranger danger is preschool/school-age teaching.

questions.question: A 9-year-old comes home alone after school while parents work. Which statement is correct?
options: This is always unsafe and should be reported.
Latchkey children may experience anxiety or develop self-care skills.
Parents should never leave children alone before age 12.
This is child neglect in all cases.
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Latchkey children (unsupervised after school) may experience anxiety, loneliness, or develop independence/self-care skills. Risk depends on child maturity, neighborhood, and preparation. A is incorrect — not always unsafe. C is incorrect — no fixed age; depends on child and situation. D is incorrect — not automatically neglect.

questions.question: Which is the leading cause of death in children over 1 year of age?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Unintentional injuries (motor vehicle accidents, drowning, poisoning, burns) are the leading cause of death after age 1 year. A is incorrect — cancer is a significant cause but not leading. B is incorrect — congenital anomalies are leading in infants <1 year. D is incorrect — infectious diseases are lower due to vaccines.

questions.question: A parent asks when their infant can face forward in the car seat. What is the correct response?
options: As soon as the infant can sit up
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Car seats should be rear-facing until the child is at least 1 year old AND 20–22 pounds (varies by seat). This protects the head, neck, and spine in a crash. A is incorrect — sitting up is not the criterion. B is incorrect — 6 months is too young. D is incorrect — 3 years is too late; most children can face forward earlier.

questions.question: Which milestones are expected in an 18-month-old toddler? (Select all that apply.)
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D): A is correct — 10–20 words by 18 months. B is correct — climbs stairs with help. D is correct — removes some clothing. C is incorrect — runs by 18 months but jumping is later (2–2.5 years). E is incorrect — drawing a circle is a 3-year milestone.

questions.question: A 5-year-old says, 'I won't hit my sister because I'll get spanked.' According to Kohlberg, this reflects which moral level?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Preconventional moral reasoning (ages ~4–7) is based on punishment and reward. The child obeys to avoid punishment. A is incorrect — postconventional is 13+ years (ethical principles). B is incorrect — conventional is 9–13 years (social approval). D is incorrect — not a moral level (Piaget cognitive stage).

questions.question: Before giving any medication to a child, what is the MOST important nursing action?
options: Ask the parent if the child likes the medication
Verify the child's weight and calculate mg/kg dose
Give the medication as quickly as possible
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Always calculate pediatric doses by weight (mg/kg) and verify against safe dose range. This prevents overdose/under-dose. A is incorrect — preference doesn't affect safety. C is incorrect — mixing with essential foods may cause refusal. D is incorrect — safety over speed.

questions.question: A parent asks when to start brushing their infant's teeth. What is correct teaching?
options: Never; baby teeth don't need brushing
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Brush teeth at first eruption (~6 months). Daily brushing removes plaque and establishes good habits. A is incorrect — too late (all teeth in by 2.5 years). C is incorrect — should start much earlier. D is incorrect — baby teeth are important for eating, speech, and space maintenance.

questions.question: A 9-month-old is beginning to pick up Cheerios using the thumb and index finger. This milestone is called:
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Pincer grasp (thumb and index finger) develops 9–12 months. It demonstrates fine motor development. A is incorrect — grasp reflex is present at birth and fades. B is incorrect — palmar grasp uses the whole hand (earlier). D is incorrect — raking grasp uses fingers together (earlier than pincer).

questions.question: Which factors influence a child's development? (Select all that apply.)
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, C, D): A is correct — heredity provides genetic blueprint. B is correct — environment (nutrition, stimulation, safety) affects development. C is correct — birth order affects (e.g., youngest may have delayed language). D is correct — temperament affects how children respond to environment. E is incorrect — income is one factor but not the only one.

questions.question: A 2-year-old wants to feed themselves with a spoon even though they make a mess. According to Erikson, this behavior supports which task?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Autonomy vs Shame and Doubt (1–3 years). Self-feeding builds independence and autonomy. The mess is normal; parents should encourage attempts. A is incorrect — trust is infant. C is incorrect — initiative is preschool. D is incorrect — industry is school-age.

questions.question: A nurse calculates a medication dose for a child. What is the MOST important step before administration?
options: Ask the parent if the dose looks right
Have another nurse verify the calculation
Mix the medication with the child's favorite food
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Double-checking pediatric medication calculations with another nurse is critical to prevent errors. Weight-based dosing (mg/kg) must be verified. A is incorrect — parents may not know safe doses. C is incorrect — safety first. D is incorrect — mixing with essential foods may cause refusal.

questions.question: Which statement about oral health is correct for children?
options: Baby teeth don't matter since they fall out
Brush teeth at bedtime and after meals
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Brush at bedtime (most important) and after meals. Baby teeth are space maintainers. A is incorrect — baby teeth are critical. C is incorrect — fluoride after 6 months prevents cavities. D is incorrect — sharing transmits bacteria.

questions.question: A 2-year-old says 'no' to everything. According to Erikson, this is:
questions.rationale: Correct (B): 'No' is normal for Autonomy vs Shame and Doubt (1–3 years). It's the child's way of asserting independence. A is incorrect — normal. C is incorrect — not hearing. D is incorrect — not a disorder.

questions.question: Which are true about development? (Select all that apply.)
options: All children develop at the exact same rate
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D, E): A is correct — growth is quantitative. B is correct — development is qualitative. D is correct — sequence is predictable. E is correct — environment matters. C is incorrect — rate varies by individual.

questions.question: A preschooler believes the moon follows them. This is:
questions.rationale: Correct (A): Magical thinking is normal in Preoperational stage (2–7 years). The child believes the moon follows them or that wishes come true. B is incorrect — not hallucination. C is incorrect — not autism sign. D is incorrect — not vision.

questions.question: A 3-year-old in the hospital is playing with a doll and pretending to give it medicine. This is:
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Therapeutic play allows children to process feelings about hospitalization and procedures. Playing doctor/nurse helps them understand and cope. A is incorrect — normal. C is incorrect — not regression. D is incorrect — not attention seeking.

questions.question: Which pain scale is appropriate for a 10-year-old?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Numeric 0–10 scale is appropriate for school-age children (6+ years) who can understand numbers. A is for nonverbal infants. B is for preschool 3+ years. D is incorrect — only C is appropriate here.

questions.question: A 2-year-old insists on dressing themselves even though they put clothes on backwards. This supports which Erikson stage?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Autonomy vs Shame and Doubt (1–3 years). Self-dressing (even if imperfect) builds independence. A is incorrect — trust is infant. C is incorrect — initiative is preschool. D is incorrect — industry is school-age.

questions.question: How often should children brush their teeth?
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Brush after each meal/snack and at bedtime. This removes plaque and prevents cavities. A is incorrect — once daily is insufficient. C is incorrect — too infrequent. D is incorrect — appearance is not the indicator.

questions.question: When should solid foods be introduced to infants?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Solids at 4–6 months when infant shows readiness (sitting with support, head control, interest in food). A is incorrect — breast/formula only 0–6 months. B is incorrect — too early. D is incorrect — solids should be well-established by 1 year.

questions.question: Which safety measures are appropriate for toddlers? (Select all that apply.)
options: Keep hot liquids out of reach
Allow unsupervised play in the street
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, C, D): A is correct — poison prevention. B is correct — burn prevention. C is correct — drowning prevention. D is correct — rear-facing car seat. E is incorrect — unsupervised street play is dangerous.

questions.question: The Denver II screening test assesses which four domains?
options: Personal-social, fine motor, language, gross motor
questions.rationale: Correct (B): The Denver II screens four domains: personal-social, fine motor-adaptive, language, and gross motor. It identifies children not meeting age-level expectations. A is incorrect — not an IQ test. C is incorrect — academic subjects are for school-age. D is incorrect — vision/hearing are separate screens.

questions.question: A 3-year-old hospitalized child refuses to eat, cries when parents leave, and regresses to bedwetting. Identify the primary cause and select the most appropriate nursing intervention.
options: Intervention: Encourage parents to stay and provide familiar comfort items
Intervention: Force the child to eat
questions.rationale: Correct (A and D): The child's behaviors (refusing to eat, crying when parents leave, regression to bedwetting) are classic signs of separation anxiety during hospitalization. The appropriate intervention is to encourage parents to stay with the child and provide familiar comfort items (blanket, stuffed animal) to reduce stress. B is incorrect — this is not nutritional deficiency. C is incorrect — no UTI symptoms. E is incorrect — restricting fluids doesn't address the emotional cause. F is incorrect — forcing the child to eat increases stress.

questions.question: A 4-year-old refuses to eat green peas, saying 'They're green and I don't like green things.' This demonstrates _____ thinking, a normal characteristic of Piaget's _____ stage (ages 2-7 years).
questions.rationale: Correct: Centration (B) and Preoperational (C). Centration is the tendency to focus on one characteristic (color) while ignoring others (taste, nutrition) — a hallmark of Piaget's Preoperational stage (2–7 years). Magical thinking (A) is also Preoperational but the specific behavior here is centration. Conservation (D) is Concrete Operational (7–11 years).

questions.question: Which statements about NGN question types are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Matrix questions require matching items in rows to columns.
SATA questions may have multiple correct answers.
Bowtie questions assess cause-effect and interventions.
Highlight questions require selecting text in a passage.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, C, D, E): All five statements are correct. Matrix questions match rows to columns. SATA (Select All That Apply) can have multiple correct answers. Bowtie questions assess cause/effect and interventions. Cloze questions use drag-and-drop to fill blanks. Highlight questions require selecting text in a passage. These are the six NGN types (matrix, bowtie, cloze, highlight, SATA, MCQ).

questions.question: A parent is teaching about car seat safety. Highlight the THREE correct statements that should be included in teaching.
options: Infants should be rear-facing until 1 year AND 20-22 pounds minimum.
Children can face forward as soon as they can sit up.
Never place a rear-facing car seat in front of an airbag.
Booster seats are for children under 40 pounds.
Car seat should be installed in the back seat, never the front.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, C, E): Rear-facing until 1 year AND 20-22 pounds (A), never place rear-facing seat in front of airbag (C), and always in back seat (E) are correct. B is incorrect — sitting up alone is not the criterion. D is incorrect — booster seats are for children 40+ pounds (after outgrowing car seat).

questions.question: How many baby (deciduous) teeth do children have?
questions.rationale: Correct (C): Children have 20 baby teeth, which are typically all present by 2.5 years of age. These are eventually replaced by 32 permanent teeth. A is incorrect — 10 is half. B is incorrect — 16 is not a standard count. D is incorrect — 32 is the permanent adult count.

questions.question: Which weight milestones are correct for infants? (Select all that apply.)
options: Weight gain of 5-7 oz/week in first 6 months
Weight gain of 3-5 oz/week in second 6 months
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, D, E): Birth weight doubles by 5–6 months (A), triples by 12 months (B), 5-7 oz/week first 6 months (D), 3-5 oz/week second 6 months (E). C is incorrect — birth weight quadruples by about 30 months, not 24.

questions.question: Which statement best describes cephalocaudal development?
options: Development proceeds from center to periphery
Development proceeds from head to toe
Development proceeds from general to specific
Development is the same in all children
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Cephalocaudal development means head-to-toe (cephalo = head, caudal = tail). Infants gain head control before sitting, walking, etc. A is incorrect — that is proximodistal (center-to-periphery). C is incorrect — that is general-to-specific (motor development). D is incorrect — development varies by individual.

questions.question: Which statement best describes proximodistal development?
options: Development proceeds from head to toe
Development proceeds from center to periphery
Development proceeds from general to specific
Development is the same in all children
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Proximodistal development means center-to-periphery (proximo = near, distal = far). Infants use whole arm reach before fine finger grasp. A is incorrect — that is cephalocaudal (head-to-toe). C is incorrect — that is general-to-specific (motor development). D is incorrect — development varies by individual.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching about family types. Which statements are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: A nuclear family includes parents and their biological children.
A blended family includes children from previous relationships.
An extended family includes grandparents, aunts, and uncles.
A single-parent family is not considered a family type.
A foster family is a temporary placement for children.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, C, E): A is correct — nuclear family is parents + biological children. B is correct — blended family includes stepchildren. C is correct — extended family includes relatives. E is correct — foster families are temporary placements. D is incorrect — single-parent families are a valid family type.

questions.question: A 2-year-old hospitalized child is crying, hitting, and pushing away staff when parents leave. Identify the cause and select the most appropriate intervention.
options: Cause: Protest stage of separation anxiety
Intervention: Encourage parents to stay with child as much as possible
Intervention: Restrain the child during procedures
questions.rationale: Correct (A and D): The child's behavior (crying, hitting, pushing away staff) is the Protest stage of separation anxiety — the first stage. The appropriate intervention is to encourage parents to stay with the child as much as possible. B is incorrect — no signs of abuse. C is incorrect — not hearing. E is incorrect — restraint increases stress. F is incorrect — ignoring increases anxiety.

questions.question: Compared to adults, infants have a higher metabolic rate. What is the clinical implication?
options: Infants need fewer calories per kg
Infants need more calories and fluids per kg
Infants can tolerate long periods without eating
questions.rationale: Correct (B): Infants have HIGHER metabolic rates per kilogram than adults, meaning they require more calories and fluids proportionally. This is why infants need frequent feedings. A is incorrect — opposite. C is incorrect — infants cannot tolerate long periods without eating. D is incorrect — infants have higher oxygen needs.

questions.question: Which statements about play and development are correct? (Select all that apply.)
options: Parallel play is characteristic of toddlers (1-3 years).
Cooperative play is characteristic of preschoolers (3-5 years).
Competitive play with rules is characteristic of school-age children (6-12 years).
Fantasy play is characteristic of infants (0-12 months).
Solitary play is the primary play type for infants.
questions.rationale: Correct (A, B, C, E): A is correct — parallel play is toddlers. B is correct — cooperative play is preschoolers. C is correct — competitive play is school-age. E is correct — solitary play is infants. D is incorrect — fantasy play is preschoolers (3-5 years), not infants.

questions.question: A 4-year-old says 'I won't hit my sister because I'll get spanked.' This reflects _____ moral reasoning, which is characteristic of Kohlberg's _____ level (ages 4-7 years).
questions.rationale: Correct: Preconventional (A) and Preconventional (A). The child obeys to avoid punishment — this is Preconventional moral reasoning (Kohlberg's first level, ages 4-7 years). Conventional (B) is 9-13 years (social approval). Postconventional (C) is 13+ years (ethical principles). Formal (D) is Piaget's cognitive stage, not Kohlberg.

questions.question: Match each milestone with the correct age:
questions.rationale: Correct mapping: Pincer grasp (9-12 months) → C. Independent walking (12-15 months) → D. First words (10-12 months) → C. Stranger anxiety peaks (6-8 months) → B. This tests knowledge of developmental milestones in the first year.
